county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1857 from Crawford. County seat: Rolla. Ozark Highlands region with rugged terrain and Southern Appalachian settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Rolla holds land records from 1857. Phelps County is home to Missouri University of Science and Technology (Missouri S&T, formerly School of Mines) in Rolla, which has archives with local history materials. Phelps County Historical Society has genealogical files. Route 66 heritage — Rolla was an important stop. Fort Leonard Wood (Pulaski County, adjacent) brought military families to the region. Check Crawford County for pre-1857 records. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate. Missouri State Archives holds circuit court records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1857

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1857 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records intact from county formation (1857)

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, administrations, and guardianship records from 1857. Missouri probate courts also handle some adoption records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and records from 1857. Missouri marriage records kept at county level by Recorder of Deeds. FamilySearch has indexed many Missouri marriages.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1818 from St. Charles. County seat: Bowling Green. Part of Missouri's 'Little Dixie' — strong Southern heritage and slaveholding region.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Bowling Green holds land records from 1818. Pike County is on the Mississippi River in northeast Missouri. Pike County Historical Society has genealogical files. Clarksville was an important river port. Pike County attracted early Virginia and Kentucky settlers — strong Southern heritage with significant enslaved persons records. Check St. Charles County for pre-1818 records. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate. Missouri State Archives holds circuit court records. Louisiana (MO) was named for the Louisiana Purchase and has early settlement records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1818

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1838 from Platte Purchase. County seat: Platte City. Part of the Platte Purchase (1836) — last major land acquisition from Native Americans in Missouri.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Platte City holds land records from 1838. Platte County was part of the Platte Purchase (1836), the last major land acquisition in Missouri from Native American tribes. Pre-1836 records are federal Indian agency files at NARA-KC. Platte County Historical Society in Platte City has genealogical files. Kansas City International Airport is in Platte County. Strong Southern settlement with significant enslaved persons records. The county is now largely suburban Kansas City. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate. Missouri State Archives holds early land entry records.

county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Bolivar holds land records from 1835. Polk County Historical Society in Bolivar has genealogical files. Southwest Baptist University in Bolivar has some local history collections. Check Greene County for pre-1835 records. Ozark fringe county with Southern settlement heritage from Tennessee and Kentucky. Some Civil War guerrilla activity but courthouse survived. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate. Missouri State Archives holds circuit court records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1833 from Crawford. County seat: Waynesville. Ozark Highlands region with rugged terrain and Southern Appalachian settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Waynesville holds land records from 1833. Pulaski County is home to Fort Leonard Wood, a major US Army installation since 1940, which has dramatically affected the county's demographics. Pre-Fort Wood settlement was sparse Ozark farming communities. Pulaski County Historical Society has genealogical files. Route 66 heritage through Waynesville and Devil's Elbow. Check Crawford County for pre-1833 records. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate. Missouri State Archives holds circuit court records. Fort Leonard Wood Museum has military installation history.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1820 from Pike. County seat: New London. Part of Missouri's 'Little Dixie' — strong Southern heritage and slaveholding region.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in New London holds land records from 1820. Ralls County is on the Mississippi River in northeast Missouri — one of Missouri's oldest counties. Ralls County Historical Society has genealogical files. Mark Twain connections — Clemens family was in this area. New London served as an early river port. Strong Virginia and Kentucky settlement heritage. Check Pike County for pre-1820 records. Significant enslaved persons records in probate files. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate. Missouri State Archives holds circuit court records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1829 from Chariton. County seat: Huntsville. Part of Missouri's 'Little Dixie' — strong Southern heritage and slaveholding region.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Huntsville holds land records from 1829. Randolph County is in the 'Little Dixie' region of central Missouri — strongly pro-Southern with significant slaveholding. Randolph County Historical Society in Moberly has genealogical files. Moberly was a major railroad hub (Wabash Railroad). Check Chariton County for pre-1829 records. Strong Virginia and Kentucky settlement. Enslaved persons records in probate files are extensive for this Little Dixie county. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate. Missouri State Archives holds circuit court records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1820 from Howard. County seat: Richmond. Historic Boonslick region — earliest area of American settlement west of St. Louis.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Richmond holds land records from 1820. Ray County is one of Missouri's oldest counties in the Missouri River valley. Ray County Historical Society in Richmond has genealogical files. The county was a center of the 1838 Missouri-Mormon War — Haun's Mill refugees fled through Ray County. Check LDS Church History Library for Mormon-related records. Strong Southern settlement from Virginia and Kentucky. Significant enslaved persons records. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate. Missouri State Archives holds circuit court records.

county_metadata.record_losses: 1872 fire destroyed courthouse; most pre-1872 records lost
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1845 from Shannon. County seat: Centerville. Ozark Highlands region with rugged terrain and Southern Appalachian settlement. Missouri Lead Mining District — lead mining from French colonial era to present. Burned county: 1872 fire destroyed courthouse; most pre-1872 records lost
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Centerville holds records restarting after the 1872 courthouse fire. For pre-1872 research, check Shannon County (parent), Missouri State Archives, and BLM GLO land patents. Reynolds County is a remote Ozark county with sparse population. Black River area settlement history. Strong Southern Appalachian settlement heritage. Lead and iron mining supplemented subsistence farming. Reynolds County Historical Society has limited post-fire files. FamilySearch microfilm covers post-fire records. Johnson's Shut-Ins State Park area.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1845
